# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on self-hosted runners in our datacenter. The workflow executes custom build scripts and deployment commands provided by developers. This gives our team flexibility to run any build process they need on our infrastructure.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cicd security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Workflow for GitHub Actions 
on: [push] # Triggers on any push to your repository and branches  
jobs:   
  ${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.node-version }} :    
      runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}       
       with:        
          node-version: 12              
           # You may specify custom Node.js version in .github/workflows/main.yml  
    strategy:            
            matrix:             
                npmPackageManager: [yarn, pnpm]    
                 os : ["ubuntu", "macOS"]     
                  node-version: [12.x, 14.x, 16.x ]       # List of allowed Node versions
